purpose: Registry-owned steward record for making roadmap work contract-bound, taint-aware, module-owned, and auditable before autonomous execution.

project_steward.mission: Build a private, locally trained daily-use Theseus assistant with governed self-improvement, zero external inference at runtime, honest public calibration, and evidence-backed replacement of implementations.
non_goals: Runtime serving of externally generated tokens

forbidden: public benchmark payloads into training rows

allowed_work: Create new surfaces only with registry entries
Compile roadmap items into project work contracts
Record event taint before untrusted material can influence planning or training
forbidden_work: Score fishing consumed public benchmark surfaces
Counting deterministic renderers/tools/routers/templates as learned generation
Deleting evidence without retention or archive manifest
Creating product surfaces outside current roadmap priorities
sunset_criteria: A surface is deprecated or retired when it is superseded by a registered implementation, lacks fresh evidence, or duplicates a better-owned surface without a compatibility role.
A report family is compacted when it stops serving active claims, regression evidence, or residual mining.
A module is blocked when it expands authority, loses evidence traceability, or cannot pass its validation command.

project_work_contracts.objective: Implement the roadmap's first revised-order item: project steward record, active work-contract coverage, event-taint intake, module DoD cards, and stale report-family steward decisions through the existing project registry.
required_outputs: updated project-state note if validation succeeds
evidence_requirements: project_steward_status is GREEN or explicit YELLOW with no hard gaps
active_project_work_contract_count >= 1
mandatory event-taint classes covered
module cards cover active major surfaces
steward_decision_count >= 5
project_work_contracts.rollback_path: Revert only this steward config/registry integration patch or mark project_steward_records blocked; do not delete unrelated evidence.
non_claims: This does not improve model capability by itself.
This does not prove public transfer.
This does not authorize arbitrary autonomous execution.

project_work_contracts.objective: Turn verifier-labelled strict-generator failures into a governed private preference/RLVR policy-optimization baseline without public benchmark training or deterministic renderer credit.
forbidden_tools: public benchmark payloads as training rows
acceptance_tests: private verifier pass improves or the update remains quarantined
evidence_requirements: public_training_rows=0
external_inference_calls=0
negative results preserved
rollback checkpoint recorded
project_work_contracts.rollback_path: Keep prior checkpoint as default and record failed update as residual.
non_claims: Preference/RLVR improvement is not public benchmark transfer.
Loss improvement alone is not capability.

project_work_contracts.objective: Turn generation-mode and speed work into governed accepted-output accounting, separating proposed throughput, accepted candidates, verifier success, fallback burden, memory, and no-cheat counters.
forbidden_tools: public benchmark payloads as training rows
evidence_requirements: public_training_rows=0
external_inference_calls=0
faster routes are not promotable if verifier pass or integrity regresses
raw throughput is separated from accepted output and task success
project_work_contracts.rollback_path: Remove generation-mode registry/gate references from model_governance_gates and keep prior decode/fanout reports as historical evidence.
non_claims: Generation-mode accounting does not improve model capability by itself.
VCM descriptor reuse is not native model KV-cache parity.
Candidate throughput is not verifier success.

project_work_contracts.objective: Represent concrete Theseus repo/generator repairs as semantic atoms, semantic patches, deterministic effect logs, and localized repair fixtures under the plan/execution spine.
forbidden_tools: public benchmark payloads as training rows
evidence_requirements: all semantic atoms have source anchors
all patches have validation commands and rollback notes
all effect logs declare allowed and forbidden effects
scope-changing repair emits a ledger or stays within scope
project_work_contracts.rollback_path: Remove semantic patch files from the plan compiler registry surface and retain generated reports as historical evidence.
non_claims: Semantic patches do not make syntactic success count as semantic correctness.
This does not improve model capability by itself.
This does not authorize broader tool effects.

project_work_contracts.objective: Add VCM verification-bandwidth and context adequacy governance: chunk scoring, mission brief omissions, taint/SCIF handling, deletion closure, and fail-closed context decisions.
forbidden_tools: public benchmark payloads as training rows
speculative prefetch influencing generation before promotion
evidence_requirements: mission brief records omissions and authority limits
tainted external notes cannot self-pin
deletion closure emits closed or fault states
VCM context governance makes no native KV-cache parity claim
project_work_contracts.rollback_path: Remove VCM context-governor files from the VCM registry surface and keep generated reports as historical evidence.
non_claims: Context governance is not native KV-cache parity.
Context adequacy does not prove code-generation transfer.
Mission briefs do not expand authority.

project_work_contracts.objective: Add Personal Hive policy-first scheduling records and gate: device resource cards, portal cards, job contracts, bids after policy filtering, approval receipts, and disabled-by-default federation leases.
forbidden_tools: raw private data to untrusted nodes
federation lease enablement without sandbox/revocation tests
evidence_requirements: reachable nodes can be rejected by policy
approval receipt binds one job, permission, and time window
federation leases are disabled by default
latency-sensitive jobs prefer policy-eligible nearby/operator routes
project_work_contracts.rollback_path: Remove Hive scheduler files from the Hive registry surface and keep generated reports as historical evidence.
non_claims: Fixture scheduling does not prove live peer reachability.
This does not enable public gateway operation.
This does not authorize arbitrary remote shell.

project_work_contracts.objective: Convert repeated Theseus traces into procedural tool candidates with explicit invariants, parameters, preconditions, postconditions, monitoring, regressions, lifecycle state, and retirement criteria.
forbidden_tools: toolification counted as learned model capability
route without regression or retirement criteria
evidence_requirements: at least one repeated workflow becomes a candidate
failed regression blocks route
benchmark/eval workflows are not answer tools
retirement criteria are present
default route adoption requires canary execution and an armed regression guard
project_work_contracts.rollback_path: Remove procedural-memory gate files from the plan compiler registry surface and keep generated reports as historical evidence.
non_claims: Procedural tool candidates are not learned model capability.
Candidates are not default routes unless separate registry eligibility passes.
Benchmark/eval workflows remain residual pressure, not answer tools.

project_work_contracts.objective: Make Circle/Coil/cyclic theorem-backed contracts first-class proof-boundary fixtures with theorem IDs, Lean artifact status, fingerprints, explicit non-claims, claim decisions, and substrate adoption records that cannot be credited as model-quality evidence without separate Theseus task results.
forbidden_tools: finite proof credited as ASI evidence
cyclic substrate default without transformer/hybrid baseline
model-quality claim without separate task evidence
evidence_requirements: Circle-derived fixture claims are allowed only inside their theorem boundary
model-quality/speed/memory/transfer claims require separate Theseus task evidence
Lean contract artifact status is checked before proof-carrying claims are enabled
substrate adoption records include baselines, negative controls, falsification criteria, residuals, and non-claims
project_work_contracts.rollback_path: Remove proof-carrying gate files from the deterministic tool substrate surface and retain generated reports as non-claim historical evidence.
non_claims: Circle theorem fixtures do not prove Theseus learned-model quality.
Structural proxy benchmarks do not prove public transfer or ASI progress.
Cyclic/Coil/RoPE ideas do not bypass transformer/hybrid baselines.

project_work_contracts.objective: Enforce the ASI Stack book-quality definition of done for every major Theseus registry surface through normalized module records, source crosswalks, validation commands, evidence refs, non-claims, deprecation routes, report-family caps, and steward decision linkage.
forbidden_tools: module card treated as capability evidence
stale module hidden without steward decision
duplicate report family growth without cap
evidence_requirements: all major surfaces have module records
book/charter source crosswalks are present
negative evidence remains linked through steward decisions
report-family cap and stale latest-view policy are checked
project_work_contracts.rollback_path: Remove DoD gate files from the project registry surface and keep generated module reports as quality evidence.
non_claims: Module DoD health is not learned-model capability.
A ready module card does not prove the module improves public transfer.
Book crosswalks are implementation guidance, not task performance.

event_taint_records.training_use_policy: only private/governed fields admitted by data policy
accepted metadata only unless governed data admission

residuals: exact consumed surface cannot be rerun for fishing

module_cards.problem: Roadmap work needs steward-owned contracts, taint records, module DoD, and cleanup decisions before autonomous execution.
invariants: roadmap work has a project work contract
untrusted inputs carry taint before planning/training
steward decisions cannot promote capabilities by themselves
failure_modes: sidecar steward docs ignored by registry
tainted external text becomes control text
module_cards.minimal_implementation: One steward config with active work contracts, event-taint records, module cards, and steward decisions reported by the registry gate.
non_claims: not model capability
not public transfer
module_cards.deprecation_route: merge into project_manifest_registry only after steward fields become native registry sections

module_cards.problem: Theseus needs a current self-model for active surfaces, abstractions, implementations, and cleanup governance.
module_cards.minimal_implementation: Materialized project registry and gate summary.
non_claims: registry health is not model capability
module_cards.deprecation_route: retire only after a successor registry can materialize equivalent or stricter fields

module_cards.problem: Current evidence must turn into bounded next-work decisions without bypassing gates.
module_cards.interface: scripts/theseus_control_plane.py consumes reports and emits decision reports.
failure_modes: green-looking nearby artifact avoids real blocker
module_cards.minimal_implementation: Control-plane report with evidence refs and non-claims.
non_claims: next-work decision is not task completion
module_cards.deprecation_route: replace via SCF replacement transaction

module_cards.problem: Goals need typed DAGs, VCM context, tool/evidence records, and replayable execution traces.
invariants: planning does not imply execution authority
typed jobs preserve evidence and context refs
module_cards.minimal_implementation: Compiled DAG and VIEA trace bundle.
non_claims: successful planning is not model skill
module_cards.deprecation_route: replace only through planning_execution_spine implementation replacement

module_cards.problem: Exact math/search/proof tools should compute/check evidence instead of being credited as learned generation.
invariants: tool output is evidence, not learned generation
failure_modes: tool use hidden inside model score
module_cards.minimal_implementation: Registered tool cards, tool traces, and tool-on/off private ablation.
non_claims: tool-assisted result is reported separately from model-only
module_cards.deprecation_route: retire individual tools through deterministic_tool_contract replacement

module_cards.problem: Candidate claims must be recomputed independently from code/provenance/source path, not self-declared row flags.
invariants: self-declared learned/fallback/template flags are not trusted
public artifacts cannot enter training rows
module_cards.minimal_implementation: Candidate integrity report and blind information-flow audit.
non_claims: integrity audit does not prove semantic pass
module_cards.deprecation_route: only replace with an audit that is stricter and independent

module_cards.problem: Evidence needs durable indexing and latest-view reports instead of loose report sprawl.
invariants: reports are evidence records, not progress
support states remain separate from claims
module_cards.minimal_implementation: SQLite evidence store and report evidence summary.
non_claims: indexed evidence does not mean evidence is strong
module_cards.deprecation_route: migrate with lossless evidence export

module_cards.problem: Generated artifacts, stale docs, duplicate families, and deprecated surfaces need non-destructive cleanup.
module_cards.minimal_implementation: Workspace hygiene and deprecation reports.
non_claims: cleanup is not capability
module_cards.deprecation_route: replace through project_self_model_registry

module_cards.problem: Technical debt must produce concrete maintenance packets before autonomy magnifies source sprawl.
invariants: maintenance packets cite files and roles
module_cards.minimal_implementation: ATTD report and maintenance packets.
non_claims: ATTD green is not model capability
module_cards.deprecation_route: replace only with stricter deterministic debt analyzer

module_cards.problem: Unattended work needs launch readiness, watchdog state, and bounded executor contracts.
module_cards.interface: autonomy supervisors and Hive work-board executor.
failure_modes: overnight loop launches with stale profile
module_cards.minimal_implementation: Readiness report and watchdog report.
non_claims: running longer is not improving
module_cards.deprecation_route: fold into core control plane if duplicate

module_cards.problem: Mac/Windows/Linux resource routing must be accurate and backend-specific without stale cross-machine assumptions.
module_cards.interface: resource policy, CUDA/MLX/Metal doctors, and native kernels.
invariants: speed claims count verified useful work
module_cards.minimal_implementation: Resource policy report and backend diagnosis.
non_claims: kernel parity requires apples-to-apples evidence
module_cards.deprecation_route: replace by resource_acceleration_contract transaction

module_cards.problem: Capability claims need maturity, promotion, safety, and public-transfer gates.
invariants: private 1.0s do not imply public transfer
module_cards.minimal_implementation: Gate reports with blockers and non-claims.
non_claims: gate pass is scoped to its evidence
module_cards.deprecation_route: replace only with stricter gate coverage

module_cards.problem: Public benchmark measurement must be frozen, decontaminated, consumed once, and never admitted to training.
module_cards.interface: public calibration contracts and run registry.
invariants: exact consumed surfaces not rerun for fishing
module_cards.minimal_implementation: Public benchmark contract and run registry.
non_claims: public calibration is measurement, not training
module_cards.deprecation_route: replace by stricter benchmark_ratchet_contract

module_cards.problem: The practical transformer/hybrid code generator must learn real prompt/signature-conditioned bodies without fallback/template/tool credit.
module_cards.interface: neural seed, strict MLX generator, verifier, replay, and candidate evidence scripts.
failure_modes: LM loss improves but behavior does not
module_cards.minimal_implementation: Private training/eval reports with verifier labels and integrity audit.
non_claims: private replay pass is not public transfer
guarded generation is not unconstrained learned generation
module_cards.deprecation_route: survival lane may replace SymLiquid if matched evidence wins

module_cards.problem: VCM must be the default context ABI with taint, adequacy, transactions, snapshots, and deletion closure.
invariants: context does not erase authority labels
failure_modes: more context mistaken for verified context
module_cards.minimal_implementation: VCM bridge report and context transaction reports.
non_claims: VCM semantic cache is not native KV parity
module_cards.deprecation_route: replace through context_memory_contract

module_cards.problem: Teacher and data admission must support learning without contaminating runtime or public calibration boundaries.
module_cards.interface: teacher oracle, distillation manifest, dogfood bridge, data admission policies.
invariants: teacher rows are verifier-accepted before admission
teacher tokens never serve at runtime
failure_modes: teacher output silently becomes runtime answer
module_cards.minimal_implementation: Teacher manifest/gate and data admission reports.
non_claims: teacher proposal is not model self-improvement until admitted and trained
module_cards.deprecation_route: replace through teacher_data_governance_contract

module_cards.problem: The local assistant needs useful daily interactions with VCM, tool evidence, dogfood metadata, and no runtime external inference.
invariants: dogfood raw text off by default
failure_modes: assistant looks useful but emits no training pressure
module_cards.minimal_implementation: Assistant e2e report and dogfood metadata events.
non_claims: e2e toy cases are not ChatGPT-level usefulness
module_cards.deprecation_route: replace through assistant_runtime_contract

module_cards.problem: Hive install/apps must be auditable, node-discoverable, and policy-first before distributed work is trusted.
module_cards.minimal_implementation: Hive readiness and installer artifact reports.
non_claims: LAN discovery is not secure roaming
module_cards.deprecation_route: replace through hive_operations_contract

module_cards.problem: Rust core must remain buildable and not hide old selector/template machinery behind practical claims.
module_cards.minimal_implementation: Cargo build/check plus candidate-family audit.
non_claims: build pass is not capability
module_cards.deprecation_route: split/retire modules through Rust surface owner

module_cards.problem: Docs must stay current, consolidated, and claim-bounded instead of becoming historical clutter.
invariants: PROJECT_STATE is current wall not historical dump
module_cards.minimal_implementation: Doc link/state audit and current project state.
non_claims: documentation does not prove implementation
module_cards.deprecation_route: move superseded docs to deprecated or archive with pointer

module_cards.problem: Configs must express active policy and parameters without orphaning obsolete modes.
invariants: new config variants name successor relationship
module_cards.minimal_implementation: Registry classification and config validation through owning scripts.
non_claims: a config option is not implemented behavior
module_cards.deprecation_route: mark retained/deprecated in registry and remove from active routes

module_cards.problem: Benchmark cards must define calibration surfaces without becoming training payloads.
module_cards.minimal_implementation: Benchmark card validation and run registry.
non_claims: benchmark card existence is not benchmark performance
module_cards.deprecation_route: retire cards through benchmark ratchet record

source_sync_review_decisions.phase_title: Teacher And Data Governance
source_sync_review_decisions.reason: The current local AI_book data-engine chapter and controlled completeness intake were reviewed against Theseus. Phase 7 and the roadmap matrix now require per-candidate admission receipts, lifecycle lineage, semantic contamination controls, continual-learning policy comparison, recursive-synthetic coverage diagnostics, and descendant deletion closure. The other provisional book candidates are routed into existing phases with activation conditions and remain planning-only.
non_claims: book roadmap candidates are planning obligations, not implemented Theseus capability
book source text is not training data
data-admission receipt completeness is not data quality, privacy, verified unlearning, or model capability evidence
future-candidate routing does not create active implementation lanes or block unrelated bounded private training

source_sync_review_decisions.reason: The updated Virtual Context ABI chapter adds an explicit resolver fixture set for valid leased materialization, mandatory miss, verification inadequacy, mount denial, and expired-lease reuse. The VCM context-governor gate and Phase 3 matrix now encode those fixtures and VIEA records.

The changed proof manifest remains a generated Lean-proof inventory with all proof targets implemented. It does not change the current Phase 9 Hive policy-first scheduling contract, which remains frozen until trusted peers are reachable for a live registered-task receipt proof.

The changed proof manifest remains a generated Lean-proof inventory with all proof targets implemented. It does not weaken or expand the Phase 12 public-calibration contract: public benchmarks remain calibration-only, consumed exact surfaces cannot be rerun, and no public payloads enter training rows.

The changed replacement/rollback chapter strengthens evaluator independence, monitor windows, rollback receipts, and replacement-theater controls. Phase 0 already owns this through the registry replacement policy, SCF routing checks, module DoD cards, and steward-decision cleanup queue; the phase remains partial until cleanup decisions are closed.

The changed evidence-states chapter reinforces support-state burdens, asymmetric downgrade, and evidence receipt separation. Phase 4 already carries candidate-integrity, blind input, generation-mode, and replay evidence obligations; it remains partial because direct learned full-body semantic quality is still the capability wall.

The changed policy-optimization chapter strengthens policy-delta receipts, behavior-change leases, proxy humility, and reward-laundering controls. Phase 5 already routes assistant traces and dogfood outcomes through the registered assistant schema; it remains partial until real accepted/missed/ignored traces exist in sufficient volume.

The changed routing and policy chapters reinforce least-capable adequate routing, route receipts, non-selection evidence, behavior-change leases, and route-laundering controls. Phase 8 already requires backend/resource receipts and fail-closed Mac acceleration routing; it remains partial until MLX stability and CUDA/MLX/Metal parity evidence are broad enough.

The changed evidence chapter and proof manifest reinforce support-state discipline without changing the public-calibration firewall. Phase 12 continues to allow governed fresh calibration surfaces while blocking training on public prompts, tests, hidden tests, solutions, traces, or answer templates.

The changed evidence-states chapter strengthens evidence-role separation and support-state transitions. Phase 14 now cites the shared VIEA materialized claim/proof/evidence view, compression records, defeater records, artifact retention manifests, and roadmap crosswalk evidence while keeping archive-pointer reconstruction as the remaining partial wall.

The changed evidence-states chapter requires stronger support-state handling for simulation claims. Phase 17 now includes the simulation-fidelity receipt suite and VIEA records for simulation contracts, fidelity records, counterfactual traces, world-adapter receipts, failure boundaries, claims, and evidence transitions.

The changed mathematical/search substrate chapter reinforces exact tool separation and substrate-neutral support for practical generation. Phase 10 already keeps deterministic tools separate from learned-generation credit and names the transformer/hybrid grammar-safe full-body generator as the next practical survival-lane patch; no roadmap contract change is needed.

The changed mathematical/search substrate chapter does not change the SymLiquid verdict contract. Phase 11 still requires matched-compute, matched-data evidence and keeps SymLiquid as a protected discovery lane that cannot block the practical transformer/hybrid assistant path unless it wins repeated evidence.

The changed cyclic-memory and simulation-fidelity chapters reinforce fidelity-level typing, resource limits, counterfactual boundaries, and recurrence contracts. Phase 17 already requires simulation_contract, fidelity_record, counterfactual_trace, world_adapter_receipt, failure_boundary, claim, and evidence records; the phase remains partial until those semantics attach to a real bounded planning simulation adapter.

The changed evidence-states chapter reinforces claim support and downgrade semantics for governance assertions. Phase 18 now includes the governance-rights receipt suite with material audit, redaction/appeal, portable exit, fork-denial, artifact graph, claim, and evidence-transition records.

The changed evidence-states chapter supports the existing crosswalk rule: source changes become reviewable backlog/steward decisions, not silent claim promotion. Phase 19 remains partial because the sticky backlog rows still need review before the roadmap can be called fully implemented.

The stack chapter strengthens the rule that new mechanisms must identify owner, artifact, authority ceiling, evidence gate, and non-claim. Phase 0 already enforces that through the project registry, module DoD cards, SCF route validation, and roadmap matrix; it remains partial because cleanup-queue steward decisions are still open.

The verification-bandwidth chapter strengthens the distinction between loaded context and claim-specific adequacy. Phase 3 already requires context_adequacy, context_transaction, typed context faults, and Context ABI fixture records; the remaining wall is deployed resolver/classifier conformance over real semantic addresses.

The runtime-adapter chapter strengthens tool permissions, human approval, effect leases, and evidence capture. Phase 5 already routes assistant and dogfood traces through registered schemas and VIEA runtime/evidence records; it remains partial until real daily-use outcome traces exist in volume.

The stack chapter reinforces that the model is one component inside a governed system. Phase 10 remains correctly focused on practical learned generation: direct prompt/signature body-token syntax and behavior must improve before promotion, while old template-like routes stay excluded.

The runtime-adapter chapter strengthens the effect boundary that procedural tools must respect. Phase 15 already routes procedural candidates through tool cards, replay, guarded adoption, runtime-adapter invocation, authority receipts, costed routes, and evidence transitions.

The runtime-adapter chapter reinforces that router-selected arms still need explicit effect leases and evidence receipts. Phase 16 already emits VIEA-normalized routing, authority, VCM, runtime-adapter, resource, generation-mode, failure, artifact, claim, and residual records, while router choices carry zero learned-generation credit.

The runtime-adapter chapter strengthens effect-boundary evidence. Phase 17 already records simulation contracts, fidelity records, counterfactual traces, world-adapter receipts, failure boundaries, claims, and evidence transitions; it still needs real bounded adapters beyond fixture semantics.

The authority-boundaries chapter reinforces typed authority ceilings and governance failures. Phase 18 already carries constitutional predicates, governance rights, failure boundaries, claim/evidence transitions, route validation, and rights receipt fixtures.

The stack chapter strengthens the rule that source changes should alter contracts only through typed crosswalk, registry, and evidence updates. Phase 19 already implements sticky backlog rows, source-sync reviews, public-safe evidence pointers, and module DoD work-card routing; unresolved rows remain partial work, not silent completion.

The procedural-memory chapter tightens the trace-to-tool qualification states. Phase 1 already requires durable VIEA spine records and Phase 15 carries procedural-memory/toolification gates, so no new execution-spine contract is needed from this source change.

The command-contract chapter reinforces explicit semantic interfaces, field provenance, and contract precedence. Phase 5 already routes assistant/dogfood work through command contracts, VCM packets, authority receipts, claim ledgers, and feedback events.

The resource-budget changes clarify protected overhead, displaced costs, and budget states. Phase 8 already owns resource/cost/Mac routing; the current MLX rung replay slowness should be handled as a hot-loop optimization within that existing phase, not as a new roadmap lane.

The changed sources sharpen intent contracts, semantic representation, compression, and resource discipline. Phase 10 already keeps the practical transformer/hybrid survival lane separate from SymLiquid discovery and requires learned-generation integrity before promotion.

The proof-manifest and generate/verify/repair updates reinforce calibration-only evidence, residual honesty, and proof-record boundaries. Phase 12 already holds public calibration and residual-mining discipline; no public-data training or benchmark rerun is authorized by this review.

The source changes clarify that repeated traces become routable tools only after parameter, regression, and budget evidence. Phase 15 already implements procedural-memory/toolification records and remains partial until real repeated traces qualify tools.

The command-contract update strengthens field-level authority and failure behavior. Phase 17's simulation/fidelity work already consumes typed contracts and remains partial until real simulation adapters exist beyond fixtures.

The book structure, benchmark-ratchet, and resource-budget updates reinforce that source-sync changes become roadmap claims only through the crosswalk, steward decisions, and evidence links. Phase 19 already enforces that synchronization path and keeps unresolved rows as backlog.

The agency-rights checklist adds material-usability, review, appeal, rollback, accountable-principal, residual dependency, denial/degradation, and approval-required fields. Phase 6 deterministic tool/search substrate already routes tool effects through evidence receipts and does not need a new tool contract from this rights schema change; rights enforcement is handled by the expanded governance receipt suite.

The rights schema/fixture change strengthens material usability and accountable review paths. Phase 7 already separates teacher proposal, distillation, provenance, leakage, governance-right receipts, and no-runtime-external-token boundaries; the expanded governance suite provides the shared rights evidence without changing teacher/data-governance semantics.

The constitutional-alignment chapter adds predicate conflict, migration, self-modification, and least-sufficient-power fixture expectations. Phase 10 remains the practical neural seed survival lane and still must improve learned prompt/signature body-token generation; constitutional routing is now covered in Phase 18 without changing learned-generation promotion criteria.

The constitutional-alignment chapter strengthens replacement and self-modification constraints. Phase 11 remains the protected SymLiquid discovery verdict lane; it should keep matched-compute evidence separate from practical route selection, while constitutional replacement constraints are enforced by Phase 18 records.

The proof manifest and rights schema reinforce proof/claim boundaries and material review. Phase 12 remains calibration-only public transfer and residual-mining discipline; these source changes do not authorize public benchmark training, score fishing, or new calibration runs.

The constitutional predicate and agency-rights schema changes have implementation impact: Phase 18 now requires the governance suite to emit constitutional_predicate records and fixtures for least sufficient power, predicate conflict routing, constitutional migration, and self-modification weakening rejection.

The artifact graph schema now requires parent job, context transaction refs, semantic certificate refs, replay grade, evidence gate, residuals, and non-claims. The shared VIEA materializer now normalizes artifact graph records into that schema shape and fails the materialized view if required payload fields are missing.

The context transaction schema now requires explicit operation, snapshot, mounts, read/write sets, branch policy, taint, deletion/declassification/derivative/contradiction refs, materialization and closure state, faults, audit refs, replay boundary, and non-claims. The VIEA materializer now normalizes context transaction records into that shape and reports schema payload gaps as hard view failures.

The artifact graph chapter strengthens replay and non-claim fields. Phase 4 candidate-integrity and learned-generation accounting already consume the shared VIEA materialized artifact view; the materializer update supplies the schema shape without changing learned-generation promotion criteria.

The artifact graph schema change is handled by the shared VIEA materializer. Phase 9 Hive policy-first scheduling still requires a live reachable-peer submission proof before it can move beyond partial; the schema update does not change the network reachability wall.

The semantic IR phase depends on artifact graph records as the bridge between semantic nodes and executable evidence. The VIEA materializer now emits schema-shaped artifact graph payloads with semantic certificate refs and non-claims, so Phase 13 evidence can consume them without inventing a parallel artifact lane.

MoECOT/Octopus routing consumes both artifact graph and context transaction records. The shared materializer now schema-shapes both families and enforces zero payload gaps, preserving router non-claims and preventing route/tool evidence from being miscounted as learned generation.

Governance rights and failure-boundary records cite artifact graph support. The materializer now emits artifact graph records with replay limits, evidence gates, residuals, and non-claims, so governance receipts can cite schema-shaped artifacts instead of skeletal record IDs.

The compact-generative and compression-receipt source changes add stricter evidence-shape requirements, now handled by the shared VIEA compression/compact record profiles. Phase 5 remains the assistant/dogfood trace loop and already emits policy optimization and evidence records without changing its contract.

The deterministic tool/search substrate already keeps tool outputs as evidence, not model skill. Compression receipts and evidence transitions are now enforced by shared VIEA profiles, so Phase 6 needs no separate tool-contract change.

The compression-receipt schema does not alter teacher/data governance: teacher rows remain governed by provenance, verifier, leakage, and runtime-serving boundaries. Shared VIEA compression receipts cover report/artifact compaction separately.

Policy optimization and compression receipts are now schema-shaped in shared evidence paths. Phase 8 remains partial for the real Mac acceleration wall: production MLX/Metal stability and broad CUDA/MLX/Metal parity evidence.

Compressed-artifact and policy-optimization record shapes are enforced by VIEA, but Phase 9 remains frozen for live trusted-peer evidence. No Hive remote-execution or product-surface contract changed.

RankFold/compression source changes strengthen artifact-compression non-claims and receipt obligations. Phase 10 remains the practical neural seed lane; its real wall is still prompt/signature body-token generation, not artifact compression.

The compact-generative source change is now represented by an RMI compact_generative_record with explicit non-claims. Phase 11 still requires repeated matched-compute evidence before changing the SymLiquid discovery verdict.

Compact-generation and generate-verify-repair compression changes reinforce residual honesty and non-claim accounting. Phase 12 remains calibration-only and still forbids training on public benchmark prompts, tests, solutions, traces, or answer templates.
Compressed-artifact and evidence-transition schemas are now normalized by VIEA. Phase 13 remains focused on getting generator and verifier paths to consume the shared semantic IR vocabulary.

The evidence-transition schema change is handled by shared VIEA materialization. Phase 15 remains partial until future procedural candidates and assistant intents use the same adoption/rollback guard and route-binding contract by default.
The evidence-transition schema is already enforced through MoECOT/Octopus VIEA route records. Phase 16 remains partial for the learned sparse router head and real task-to-arm contrastive traces.

The evidence-transition schema and fixture changes are covered by the simulation fidelity suite and shared VIEA records. Phase 17 remains partial until real bounded world adapters and fidelity/resource audits exist beyond fixture semantics.

The governance/failure-boundary phase already emits evidence-transition records through the rights suite and live operator audit/export path. The remaining wall is trusted-peer artifact endpoint proof, not local evidence-transition shape.

The costed-route schema change is enforced by the shared VIEA materializer. Phase 5 assistant/dogfood traces already consume VIEA route evidence and keep dogfood/policy evidence separate from learned-generation claims.

The layer-boundary schema reinforces owner/responsibility/authority handoff fields. Phase 7 teacher/data governance already keeps teacher proposal, distillation, provenance, leakage, and runtime-serving boundaries explicit; no teacher contract change is introduced here.

The policy and costed-route updates are covered by the policy optimization gate and shared VIEA costed_route normalization. Phase 8 remains partial for actual MLX/Metal route stability and hardware parity evidence.
The proof manifest and costed-route schema reinforce policy-first scheduling and route cost boundaries. Phase 9 remains frozen until a trusted peer is reachable for live registered-task receipts.

The costed-route schema does not change the matched-compute SymLiquid verdict contract. It is enforced by shared VIEA routing evidence while Phase 11 remains blocked on repeated comparable substrate evidence.

Costed-route normalization is now shared through VIEA and supports procedural route adoption records. Phase 15 remains partial until future procedural candidates all use the adoption/rollback guard by default.

MoECOT/Octopus route records now materialize strict costed_route payloads through VIEA. Phase 16 remains partial because the learned sparse router head still needs real task-to-arm traces and contrastive negatives.

The stack/evidence chapter changes reinforce the existing crosswalk rule: AI_book source updates must become steward-reviewed backlog or implementation evidence, not silent roadmap completion. Phase 19 remains partial for unresolved backlog rows.

The Stable Capability Fields update strengthens exact identity, route-validation, lease, adaptation, migration, and governance bindings. The project registry now validates SCF public-release boolean clauses, consumes the VIEA route-validator materialized view, and fails closed on stale/missing routing evidence.
The proof-contract receipt schema change is already represented by VIEA claim/evidence/proof materialization. Phase 1 remains the canonical execution spine and needs no new sidecar path.

The SCF source changes are implemented in the project registry route validator and VIEA materialized-view receipt checks. Phase 2 remains partial only for live reachable-peer registered-task receipt proof.

The proof/evidence changes reinforce existing candidate-integrity and learned-generation accounting: independent integrity audit, no fallback/template/router/tool credit, and public-calibration-only boundaries remain unchanged.

Runtime adapter and proof receipt changes are already covered by assistant/Hive VIEA adapter receipts, authority receipts, and governance audit records. Phase 5 remains partial for real dogfood usage pressure, not schema shape.
Deterministic tool outputs already enter evidence transitions and proof receipts through the shared VIEA spine. No new solver/search abstraction is needed for this source revision.
The proof-contract receipt update does not change resource/cost routing. Phase 8 remains partial for actual MLX/Metal route stability and CUDA/MLX/Metal parity evidence.
Proof receipt changes reinforce the existing strict-generator no-cheat proof boundary. The practical neural seed lane remains blocked on semantic body-token behavior, not proof record shape.
Proof-carrying-claim and proof-contract revisions are already normalized through report evidence, VIEA claim ledger entries, proof/receipt non-claims, and compression/defeater records.

Runtime adapter and evidence-transition changes are covered by procedural memory/toolification VIEA records. Phase 15 remains partial until real repeated trajectories become adoption/rollback-guarded tools.

MoECOT/Octopus routing already emits runtime adapter, authority, context, costed-route, generation-mode, failure-boundary, artifact, claim, evidence-transition, and residual records through VIEA. The remaining wall is learned sparse router traces.
Simulation/fidelity proof and adapter changes are covered by the simulation fidelity suite and shared VIEA evidence/proof records. Real bounded world adapters remain future work.

Governance rights and failure-boundary records already emit evidence transitions through the rights suite and operator governance audit/export path. The remaining wall is trusted-peer artifact endpoint proof.

AI_book release-record schema and fixture edits affect Phase 2 route-authority/source-release metadata. The current SCF abstraction/implementation registry and release-authority receipt surfaces already carry the required authority boundary; no new implementation surface or roadmap matrix change is needed.

AI_book semantic atom schema/fixture edits reinforce the existing Phase 13 semantic IR contract. Theseus already materializes semantic_atom and semantic_node records through the shared VIEA view; the remaining Phase 13 wall is consuming that vocabulary in generator/verifier obligations, not a schema change.

The cognitive-compilation source update is already represented by Phase 15 procedural-memory/toolification gates and guarded adoption records. The remaining work remains applying the same route binding to future replay-passed candidates, not changing the current contract.

The integrated-reference and reference-trace source updates reinforce the existing VIEA artifact/claim/evidence trace contract. Theseus already materializes shared VIEA records and public-safe evidence pointers; no new Phase 5 contract is needed beyond preserving reference-trace discipline in the existing spine.

Phase 12 was updated from prose-only calibration discipline to a VIEA-gated public-calibration proposal contract. The gate cites candidate integrity, training-data firewall state, alignment preflight, and exact run-registry consumed/fresh-surface state before any public measurement can count as evidence.

The compact-generative schema added generation, verification, fallback, residual-burden, promotion-blocker, and source-ref fields. Theseus now emits those fields from the RMI compact-generative record and enforces them in the VIEA materialized-view schema gate.

source_sync_review_decisions.phase_title: MoECOT And Octopus Router Integration
source_sync_review_decisions.reason: Phase 16 AI_book reader-manuscript drift for runtime adapters and permissions was reviewed against the current Octopus/MoECOT router contract. The registered router path already separates routing decisions from learned-generation claims and emits route receipts; the next Phase 16 work remains improving policy quality through evidence, not adding a new router or permission lane.
non_claims: source drift review is not routing-quality evidence
book source text is not training data
router receipts do not count as learned code generation
clearing source-sync backlog does not complete partial implementation phases

source_sync_review_decisions.phase_title: VIEA Execution Spine
source_sync_review_decisions.reason: Phase 1 AI_book reader-manuscript procedural-memory drift was reviewed against the current VIEA execution-spine contract. The current spine already emits typed job, artifact graph, claim/proof/evidence, authority, runtime-adapter, failure-boundary, context, and procedural canary/adoption records; the unresolved Phase 1 wall remains making normal assistant defaults and live reachable-peer registered-task receipts traverse that spine, not adding a new procedural sidecar.
non_claims: source drift review is not model capability evidence
book source text is not training data
procedural-memory prose does not authorize unbounded tool execution
clearing source-sync backlog does not complete Phase 1 while live execution proof remains missing

source_sync_review_decisions.phase_title: Virtual Context Memory As Default Context Substrate
source_sync_review_decisions.reason: Phase 3 AI_book reader-manuscript procedural-memory drift was reviewed against the current VCM context ABI. VCM already binds procedural/tool/verifier/assistant context through governed context_transaction, context_adequacy, resolver faults, leases, artifact refs, taint boundaries, and no-raw-payload defaults; the remaining VCM runtime work stays routed through native KV/prefix lifecycle in Phase 8.
non_claims: source drift review is not VCM benchmark evidence
book source text is not training data
context ABI receipts are not model-native KV/prefix parity
clearing source-sync backlog does not create a new memory benchmark claim

source_sync_review_decisions.phase_title: Procedural Memory And Toolification
source_sync_review_decisions.reason: Phase 15 AI_book reader-manuscript procedural-memory drift was reviewed against the current procedural-memory implementation. Theseus already mines repeated assistant traces into candidate procedural memories, runs a bounded replay canary, requires route-binding contracts, arms regression guards, and adopts only through a guarded registry transaction. Future work should reuse that contract rather than add another procedural-memory lane.
non_claims: source drift review is not procedural-memory utility evidence
book source text is not training data
procedural route adoption is not learned code generation
clearing source-sync backlog does not authorize unbounded tool execution

source_sync_review_decisions.phase_title: Virtual Context Memory As Default Context Substrate
source_sync_review_decisions.reason: Phase 3 AI_book benchmark-ratchet prose drift was reviewed against the current VCM context and evidence contract. The changed chapter reinforces evidence-class separation, residual retention, public-calibration boundaries, and anti-Goodhart review discipline. The current VCM implementation already routes context packets, context adequacy, benchmark/public-memory quarantine, taint boundaries, and non-claim receipts through registered gates; no new VCM contract is needed beyond keeping VCM evidence typed and calibration-only where applicable.
non_claims: source drift review is not VCM benchmark evidence
book source text is not training data
VCM public-memory benchmarks remain calibration-only unless governed evidence packets say otherwise
clearing source-sync backlog does not claim model-native KV/prefix parity or broad memory benchmark dominance

source_sync_review_decisions.phase_title: Daily-Use Assistant Runtime And Dogfood Trace Loop
source_sync_review_decisions.reason: Phase 5 AI_book reference-trace fixture and replay-result drift was reviewed against the current assistant product-spine contract. The July 6 fixture imports the Theseus weekly-focus reference trace and book-importable evidence packs through the existing reference-trace harness with exact digests, public-safety boundaries, expected-invalid controls, residual/verifier/governance-tax evidence terms, and explicit non-claims. This strengthens book-facing import evidence only; it does not change the assistant product-lane implementation contract, prove real assistant usefulness, or create a new dogfood/training lane.
non_claims: source drift review is not assistant capability evidence
book source text is not training data
reference-trace fixture import does not prove deployed assistant usefulness, route quality, model quality, benchmark performance, safety, ASI, or clean live Theseus replay
clearing source-sync backlog does not create a new assistant product lane or promote chapter-core support state

source_sync_review_decisions.phase_title: Deterministic Solver, Search, And Tool-Use Spine
source_sync_review_decisions.reason: Phase 6 AI_book reference-trace fixture and replay-result drift was reviewed against the current deterministic tool substrate and evidence-record contract. The July 6 fixture includes deterministic-tool and verifier evidence only as public-safe artifact references inside the existing reference-trace harness. This does not alter the tool substrate contract, does not count tools as learned generation, and does not promote tool-assisted results as model-only capability.
non_claims: source drift review is not deterministic tool capability evidence
book source text is not training data
tool evidence references do not count as learned generation or model-only benchmark performance
clearing source-sync backlog does not create a new tool-use lane or promote chapter-core support state

steward_decisions.reason: Generated report history is evidence and residual context, but bulky variants should be compacted or archived by manifest instead of treated as source.
allowed_next_actions: run artifact retention
write archive manifest
keep latest-view reports declared by surfaces

steward_decisions.reason: Failed private generator runs explain the current semantic wall and must remain linked to claims instead of being hidden.
allowed_next_actions: mine residual classes
compact bulky candidate files with manifest
block capability promotion

steward_decisions.target: private_train_body_template_selector and structural adapter promotion paths
steward_decisions.reason: Routers/templates/renderers are useful baselines or controls but cannot support learned-generation claims.
allowed_next_actions: use as diagnostic baseline
exclude from learned-generation promotion
report separately

steward_decisions.reason: Public memory benchmark artifacts can calibrate VCM but must not flow into model training context.
allowed_next_actions: taint as calibration-only
archive bulky payloads
write deletion-closure faults if needed

steward_decisions.reason: Hive product surfaces are retained but should not outrun generator, assistant, registry, VCM, and authority-spine health.
allowed_next_actions: fix installer blockers
keep security updates
resume polish after core spine is healthy

steward_decisions.reason: Neural seed configuration variants are legitimate experiment parameters only when they extend the canonical comparator or declare a registered successor; they must not create another hidden learned-generation lane.
allowed_next_actions: merge compatible neural seed config variants into the canonical comparator
archive stale variants through the artifact retention manifest
require any successor generator to bind to the registry before routing

steward_decisions.reason: VCM paper/source/rendered variants are one reference packet, not competing roadmap surfaces; updates should land inside docs/reference/virtual_context_memory_v1.0 unless a successor packet is explicitly registered.
allowed_next_actions: keep rendered VCM variants inside the registered reference packet
link implementation changes through the VCM registry surface
reject new VCM doc lanes without a successor packet record

steward_decisions.reason: Generated report families are evidence history and should be compacted by retention manifests, not treated as source lanes or deleted in a way that breaks claim replay.
allowed_next_actions: keep declared latest-view report outputs for active surfaces
compact or archive bulky historical variants with pointer manifests
preserve negative evidence needed for current capability walls
